response.justification: This candidate is a talented generalist with 19 years of diverse software engineering experience, but does not meet the critical mandatory requirements for this role. While they demonstrate strong communication and adaptability across tech stacks, they have only ~6 months of documented C++ professional experience (vs. 5–10 years required), no Windows platform development background, and zero fixed-income or capital-markets domain expertise. The combination of missing C++ depth and lacking domain knowledge represents a fundamental skills gap that cannot be bridged within the scope of this mid-senior consulting role.
strengths: 19 years of diverse software engineering experience with demonstrated ability to learn and master multiple tech stacks
Excellent communication and mentoring abilities, evidenced by workshops, courses, and conference talks
Some financial-services exposure through RIDE Capital investment-technology work and full-stack engineering fundamentals
concerns: Only ~6 months of documented C++ professional experience (National Instruments) vs. mandatory 5–10 years; this is a critical gap
No fixed-income derivatives, capital markets, or investment-banking domain expertise; background is primarily web, mobile, and embedded systems
No evidence of Windows platform development; career focused on Linux, Node.js, web technologies, and cross-platform tools rather than Windows-native C++ systems
